site.title: NazivIER — IER researcher-title calculator
site.description: Internal tool of the Institute for Economic Research (IER, Ljubljana) for automatic calculation of researcher titles under the new rulebook, powered by SICRIS and OpenAlex.
site.ogDescription: Automatic title-eligibility check under the IER researcher-titles rulebook (draft 26 May 2026). Bibliography from SICRIS, citations from OpenAlex.

header.eyebrow: Institute for Economic Research · internal tool
header.title: NazivIER — researcher-title calculator
header.subtitle1: Automatic eligibility check against the new
header.subtitle2: (draft 26 May 2026), Annexes 2 and 3. Bibliography from SICRIS, citations from OpenAlex (covers WoS, Scopus, Crossref).

picker.pasteUrl: or 2. Paste a SICRIS URL / enter an ID
picker.invalidInput: Enter a valid SICRIS ID (e.g. 33182) or profile URL.

loading: Fetching bibliography from SICRIS and citations from OpenAlex … (a few seconds on first request, cached after).

summary.openAlexNotFound: OpenAlex record not found — please verify the name

metadata.title: Data not exposed by public sources
metadata.subtitle: Bibliography and citations are fetched automatically (SICRIS + OpenAlex). The fields below are required by the rulebook but not available in public databases — enter or confirm manually.

fields.weight10Count: Weight-1.0 achievements without typology (completed PhD mentorships, Zois/international awards)
fields.weight05Count: Weight-0.5 achievements without typology (journal editorial board, completed leadership of research project/programme)
fields.reelectionHelp: Tick if this is a re-election to the same title — the rulebook then requires at least half of every numeric threshold since the previous election.

evalCard.reelectionTooltip: Article 22(2): on re-election to the same title the candidate must meet at least half of each first-election threshold.

equivalent.head: Achievement equivalent
equivalent.body: = achievement weight × authorship factor (Rulebook, Annex 3, Notes on criteria).

weights.head: Weights
weights.body: follow Annex 3: 1.0 for Q1/Q2 articles (1A1/1A2 in COBISS), monographs, patents; 0.7 for other peer-reviewed articles; 0.5 for invited abstracts and professional monographs; 0.3 for professional articles and reports; 0.1 for popular contributions.

q12.head: Q1/Q2 status
q12.body: is determined primarily via an embedded SCImago JR snapshot keyed by journal ISSN-L (economics, finance and adjacent journals). When the ISSN is not in the snapshot, we fall back to a heuristic: 1.01/1.02 publications are ranked by impact, the top quartile is tagged Q1, the next Q2.

authorship.head: Authorship factor
authorship.body: : SICRIS bibliography export does not expose the corresponding author, so default is 0.7 (equal authorship). For an actual promotion application, the corresponding author should be confirmed manually (weight 1.0).

citations.head: Clean citations
citations.body: are auto-fetched from OpenAlex (openalex.org — aggregates WoS, Scopus, Crossref, PubMed). Self-citations are excluded: for each of the author's works we count how many citing works share the same author ID (authorships.author.id match) and subtract that from the total. Lookup goes by ORCID first, then by name filtered to the IER institution.

leadership.head: Project leadership
leadership.body: (Standard 3) is not auto-computed — entered manually using the ARIS Category-A FTE convention.

openScience.head: Open Science (Article 11, paragraph 6)
openScience.body: : scientific publications produced after Ordinance 59/23 (2024 and later) must be made available open-access in an institutional or thematic repository. We derive the status from OpenAlex (open_access.is_oa).

earlyPromotion.head: Early promotion (Article 14, paragraph 5)
earlyPromotion.body: : we flag candidates who exceed the minimum thresholds by ≥ 50 % AND meet the years-in-research-sector floor (≥ 10 yrs for stage III, ≥ 15 yrs for stage IV). Shown as a badge on each title card.

reelection.head: Re-election (Article 22, paragraph 2)
reelection.body: : when you tick »re-election« in the meta panel, every numeric threshold is halved — the rulebook requires at least half of the first-election values.

footer: Methodology per the rulebook of 26 May 2026, Annex 3. Typology per the COBISS typology of documents/works. Citations: OpenAlex (covers WoS + Scopus + Crossref). Authorship factor defaults to 0.7 (equal authorship) — set to 1.0 for first / corresponding author per publication.
